user_query:
I need you to turn a structured aria2 download plan into concrete JSON-RPC requests and a summary. Read the plan from input/download_plan.json. Then create two files:

1) output/requests.jsonl
- Write one JSON object per line (JSON-RPC 2.0 request objects) to apply the plan.
- Order and content requirements:
  a) First line: change global options via method "aria2.changeGlobalOption" with params ["token:<secret>", {global_options from the plan}].
  b) Next, add each task using method "aria2.addUri" with params ["token:<secret>", [list of URIs], {options}]. Map plan keys directly to aria2 options (e.g., dir, out, split, max-connection-per-server, max-download-limit, user-agent). Include a top-level field "alias" on each addUri request set to that task's alias so later actions can reference it.
  c) After all tasks are added, append one request per post_action in the plan order using the correct method name: "aria2.pause", "aria2.unpause", or "aria2.forceRemove". For these, set params to ["token:<secret>", "GID::<alias>"] where <alias> matches the referenced task alias. This GID::<alias> placeholder will stand for the real GID returned by addUri.
  d) For the provided input, produce exactly 7 JSON lines total (1 global option change, 3 addUri calls, 3 post-action calls).
- Each request must include the standard JSON-RPC fields: {"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": "plan", "method": "aria2.<...>", "params": [...]}. The only extra field allowed is "alias" on addUri requests.

2) output/summary.json
- Write a machine-checkable summary with this structure:
  {
    "global_options": { ... },
    "tasks": {
      "<alias>": {
        "dir": "...",
        "out": "..." (omit if not provided),
        "split": <number> (omit if not provided),
        "user-agent": "..." (omit if not provided),
        "max-connection-per-server": <number or string> (omit if not provided),
        "max-download-limit": "..." (omit if not provided),
        "uri_count": <number of URIs>
      },
      ...
    }
  }
- Preserve option names exactly as provided. Do not normalize or change the dir paths. Do not include comments.

Assume the secret in the plan must be placed as the first param string in every call as "token:<secret>". Keep method names and option keys exactly aligned with aria2 JSON-RPC conventions. Write the two files to the specified output/ paths.
